assumptions: The intragroup dividend receivable is denominated in a currency different from the relevant functional currency, so a foreign exchange exposure exists in the consolidated financial statements.
The question is limited to accounting approaches for that foreign exchange exposure that are identifiable from the provided IFRS 9 and IFRIC 16 extracts.

approaches.label: Foreign exchange remeasurement in profit or loss
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 B5.7.2 states that foreign exchange gains and losses on monetary financial assets and liabilities are recognised in profit or loss under IAS 21, subject to specific hedge-accounting exceptions.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 5.7.3 identifies hedged items in a hedging relationship and directs their gain-or-loss recognition to the hedge-accounting requirements in Section 6.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 B5.7.2 explicitly names cash flow hedge treatment as a distinct hedge-accounting outcome relevant to foreign exchange gains and losses on monetary items.

approaches.label: Net investment hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRIC 16 specifically addresses hedge accounting for foreign currency risk on a net investment in a foreign operation in consolidated financial statements, with effective hedge amounts recognised in OCI and reclassified on disposal.
